repositories.brief_summary: Week 11 saw optimization hints land in bytecode for Js_of_ocaml/Wasm_of_ocaml, faster UTF-8 normalization, several typing fixes, and runtime bug fixes.
repositories.new_features_summary: A long-awaited bytecode optimization hints feature merged, enabling Js_of_ocaml and Wasm_of_ocaml to leverage Lambda-level hints stored in .cmo/.cma files.
repositories.new_features:
- [#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438) **Bytecode optimization hints** were merged after a long development period: the bytecode compiler now preserves optimization hints from the Lambda IR (closure kinds, immutable blocks, array lengths, C call details) in a new `HINT` section in `.cmo` and `.cma` files, enabled with `-bytecode-hints`. This is intended to help [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)'s Js_of_ocaml and Wasm_of_ocaml projects generate better output by accessing type-level information that was previously discarded at the bytecode stage.
- [#14618](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14618) **Faster UTF-8 normalization** outside the ASCII range was merged, optimizing the `Misc.Stdlib` unicode normalization path. Contributed by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), reviewed by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), Daniel Bünzli, and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
repositories.activity_summary: Nine PRs were merged this week spanning typing fixes, runtime bug fixes, documentation updates, and a major bytecode feature; sixteen new PRs were opened.
repositories.activity:
- [#14626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14626) **Warning 16 false positive with module-dependent functions** was fixed by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob): the typechecker now correctly handles the case where a function type has a module-dependent parameter when determining whether an optional argument is unerasable.
- [#14638](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14638) **`is_inferred` now traverses local opens** correctly, fixing the issue reported in [#14629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14629) where a `let open` expression was preventing proper detection of optional argument elimination. Merged by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
- [#14635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14635) **`caml_floatarray_gather` control flow bug** was fixed by [Marc Lasson](https://github.com/mlasson): `Float.Array.sub`, `Float.Array.append`, and `Float.Array.concat` could return a non-empty array when the result should have been `[||]`.
- [#14645](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14645) **Ephemeron GC corner case documented** by [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez): a subtle case in ephemeron marking during the major GC was documented to prevent future confusion.
- [#14617](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14617) The **`ocaml_deprecated_cli` alert** is now made fatal in compiler development flags by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), improving enforcement of the migration away from deprecated CLI patterns.
- [#14640](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14640) A **surplus argument in the `Fun.protect` test** was fixed by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon).
- [#14648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14648) **Modular explicits: optional arguments are elided** for functional arguments to higher-order functions in the Lambda IR, opened by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) as part of ongoing modular explicit work.
- [#14646](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14646) **ARM Thumb mode**: [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) opened a fix to avoid using the `TBH` (Table Branch Halfword) instruction in Thumb mode which can cause issues on some ARM targets.
- [#14647](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14647) **Missing GC root for continuation in `reperform`** was opened by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) as a fix for the interpreter's handling of effects.
- [#14636](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14636) **Ban unsafe primitive aliases inside recursive modules** was proposed by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) to prevent potential unsoundness.
- [#14642](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14642) **Runtime cleanup-at-exit** behavior was proposed for improvement by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): `caml_shutdown` would be ignored rather than causing errors when other domains remain running.
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussion on raising the default minor heap size from 256Kwords (2MiB), with questions about what modern hardware cache sizes should inform the new default.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634) [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened a discussion on **what should be the default minor heap size**, noting that the current 256Kword (2MiB) default has not changed since 2011 and that running allocation-heavy benchmarks with `OCAMLRUNPARAM=s=2M` often produces 2x speedups. The discussion explores whether the minor heap should be sized relative to L3 cache or total RAM.
- [#14644](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14644) [Thibaut Mattio](https://github.com/tmattio) reported **bytecode heap corruption** when using `try Effect.perform with Effect.Unhandled` under a deep effect handler, a likely regression related to the continuation representation changes in [#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469).
- [#14639](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14639) [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) reported a bug where a **labelled argument of a labelled tuple gets its labels confused** in the typechecker.
- [#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14630) [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) reported a regression in 5.5 where **"The signature for this packaged module couldn't be inferred"** is incorrectly raised in the presence of recursive functions and first-class modules.
- [#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594) Ongoing discussion around **segfaults and timeouts on parallel tests** in cleanup-at-exit mode, with [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) tracking the issue and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) proposing a fix via [#14642](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14642).
repositories.trends_summary: Continued investment in the bytecode backend for JavaScript/WebAssembly targets, active modular explicits work, and growing attention to runtime stability with effects.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **bytecode backend as a vehicle for JavaScript/WebAssembly optimization** is gaining traction: [#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438) adds a new `HINT` section to bytecode files so that tools like Js_of_ocaml can access Lambda-level information (closure kinds, C call details, mutability) that was previously unavailable at the bytecode level. A new `-bytecode-hints` flag controls this feature.
- **Modular explicits** continue to receive active development with [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)'s work on eliding optional arguments in the Lambda IR ([#14648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14648)) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)'s refactoring of `Typeopt.is_function_type` ([#14631](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14631), [#14632](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14632)) to properly handle `Tfunctor`.
- **Effects and continuations stability** is an emerging focus: both the heap corruption bug ([#14644](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14644)) and the missing GC root in `reperform` ([#14647](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14647)) point to ongoing hardening of the effects runtime.
- **Typing precision improvements** from [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) (Cambridge) continue with multiple PRs open addressing let-rec approximation, polymorphic patterns, and warning 16 correctness.
repositories.contributors_summary: Contributions this week came from across the core OCaml team and external contributors, with particular activity from the typing and runtime areas.
repositories.contributors:
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) merged the bytecode optimization hints feature ([#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438)) and fixed a test issue ([#14640](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14640)).
- [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) contributed faster UTF-8 normalization ([#14618](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14618)) and opened a PR to add `-rectypes` tests ([#14643](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14643)).
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) merged fixes for warning 16 and local opens, made the deprecated CLI alert fatal, and opened modular explicit improvements.
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened the minor heap size discussion, proposed runtime cleanup-at-exit improvements, and contributed multiple typing refactors.
- [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) fixed warning 16 false positives ([#14626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14626)) and has several open PRs on let-rec approximation and polymorphic patterns.
- [Marc Lasson](https://github.com/mlasson) fixed the `caml_floatarray_gather` control flow bug ([#14635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14635)).
- [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) documented the ephemeron GC corner case ([#14645](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14645)).
- [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) opened a fix for ARM Thumb mode instruction selection ([#14646](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14646)).
- [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) opened a fix for a missing GC root in continuation reperform ([#14647](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14647)).
- [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) opened a fix for missing longident locations in the typed tree iterator/mapper ([#14649](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14649)).
- [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) proposed banning unsafe primitive aliases inside recursive modules ([#14636](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14636)).
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) opened cleanup PRs to move opnames into the bytecomp library and closed a fix for atom deserialization ([#14633](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14633)).
- [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk) added a missing reviewer credit to the Changes file.

repositories.brief_summary: Three open PRs active with review discussions, two new issues filed including two tagged as good first issues.
repositories.activity_summary: Three open PRs progressing through review, covering Windows portability, Darcs VCS cleanup, and shell PATH behaviour.
repositories.activity:
- **New PR** opened by [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) to remove the legacy `Num` patches fallback in `OpamDarcs`, eliminating code that has been superseded since Darcs 2.10.3 (2016); discussion touched on broader VCS abstraction design issues ([#6866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6866))
- **Windows portability fix** by [Virgile Robles](https://github.com/Firobe) sets `_WIN32_WINNT` to Windows 8.1 to resolve a build failure in MSYS2 environments caused by `GetCurrentProcessToken` not being available on Windows 7; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) suggested moving the flag to `configure.ac` or the dune file rather than the C source, and the change was updated accordingly ([#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862))
- **Shell hook PATH ordering** PR by [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) proposes updating the opam switch entry in PATH in-place rather than prepending it, preserving user-defined precedence; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) noted the option documentation could be clearer and flagged the potential for a breaking change for users who add paths after sourcing `opam-init.sh` ([#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859))
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) filed an issue proposing reordering the action list shown during install/upgrade so that removals appear after reinstalls, downgrades, upgrades, and installs rather than first, reducing the need to scroll to find relevant changes ([#6863](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6863))
- [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) filed a feature request for `opam admin add-conflict`, noting the subcommand is currently missing from the `opam admin` interface ([#6865](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6865))
repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussion on the Windows PR converged on placing the version flag in build configuration rather than C source.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) reviewed the **Windows `_WIN32_WINNT` fix** in [#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862) and suggested specifying the flag via `configure.ac` or the dune file (cross-referencing [ocaml/ocaml#14484](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14484)) rather than hardcoding it in the C header include, a cleaner approach that [Virgile Robles](https://github.com/Firobe) adopted
- [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) provided detailed commentary on **Darcs VCS model differences** in [#6866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6866), explaining why the current `module VCS` abstraction is Git-centric and how Darcs's context-file approach, weak hashes, and folder-based branching differ from the assumed model; [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) welcomed follow-up PRs to improve Darcs support
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) commented on [#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859) noting that the **PATH in-place update** option's documentation is misleading and that the behaviour change could be a breaking change for users with paths added after `source opam-init.sh`, recommending a prominent release note
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing attention to Windows portability and shell environment correctness, plus interest in broadening VCS support.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Windows build compatibility** continues to receive attention, with the `_WIN32_WINNT` fix in [#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862) addressing an MSYS2 build failure that surfaced through [tarides/opam-monorepo#421](https://github.com/tarides/opam-monorepo/issues/421)
- **Shell environment correctness** is an active area, with [#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859) addressing a long-standing inconsistency between interactive and non-interactive PATH ordering when using the opam shell hook
- **VCS backend quality** is seeing renewed interest, with [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal)'s Darcs PR [#6866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6866) signalling intent to improve Darcs support beyond simple dead-code removal
repositories.issues_summary: Two issues tagged as good first issues: adding `opam admin add-conflict` and supporting install/pin from a standalone opam file.
repositories.good_first_issues:
- [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) filed [#6865](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6865) requesting an **`opam admin add-conflict`** subcommand; the command is currently absent from `opam admin` and adding it would be a self-contained feature extension
- Long-standing issue [#4227](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4227) requesting **`opam install`/`opam pin` from a standalone `.opam` file** (one that contains a `url` field pointing to a source archive) was recently labelled as a good first issue by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), who noted it could be implemented as a `--from-file`/`--standalone` argument to `opam pin`
repositories.contributors_summary: Five contributors active across PRs and issues, with code review, feature requests, and VCS design discussion.
repositories.contributors:
- [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) opened feature request [#6865](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6865) for `opam admin add-conflict`
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened UI improvement issue [#6863](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6863), reviewed and commented on the PATH ordering PR [#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859), and added the `easy first issue` label to [#4227](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4227)
- [Virgile Robles](https://github.com/Firobe) opened and iterated on the Windows `_WIN32_WINNT` fix [#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862), incorporating reviewer feedback to move the flag to the dune build file
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) reviewed [#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862), suggesting a cleaner approach for specifying the Windows version flag and cross-referencing related work in [ocaml/ocaml#14484](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14484)
- [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) opened [#6866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6866) to remove legacy Darcs code and provided detailed context on Darcs's VCS model in review discussion
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) reviewed [#6866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6866) and encouraged further Darcs improvement PRs
- [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) has [#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859) open proposing in-place PATH updates for the shell hook, addressing issue [#6815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6815)

repositories.brief_summary: Issue triage around OxCaml Emacs debugger interaction; two dependabot CI PRs closed without merging.
repositories.activity_summary: Two dependabot PRs for the Nix CI action were closed without merging; an OxCaml issue was triaged.
repositories.activity:
- [#2046](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2046) opened by Dependabot to bump `cachix/install-nix-action` from 31.8.4 to 31.10.0, then closed without merging due to a noted regression in Nix 2.34.0 affecting trusted-user and netrc-file authentication for private caches.
- [#2035](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2035), an earlier Dependabot bump of the same action to 31.9.1, was closed as superseded by [#2046](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2046).
repositories.discussions_summary: Issue #2045 discusses Merlin repeatedly entering the Emacs debugger in OxCaml, traced to debug-on-error and a parser recovery exception.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Ben Lieberman](https://github.com/bhlieberman) reported in [#2045](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2045) that in Emacs with the OxCaml variant of Merlin (5.2.1-502+ox), the Lisp debugger fires repeatedly during completion; the stack trace pointed to a `force_reduction` exception in `mreader_recover.ml` and `mpipeline.ml`.
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) triaged [#2045](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2045) as likely OxCaml-specific and cc'd [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) from the OxCaml team.
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) identified the immediate trigger as Emacs `debug-on-error` being enabled, though the issue remains open as the **underlying parser recovery exception** in Merlin may still warrant investigation.
repositories.trends_summary: Dependabot CI dependency updates continue to be generated but are being deferred due to upstream regressions.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Two consecutive **Dependabot bumps for `cachix/install-nix-action`** ([#2035](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2035), [#2046](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2046)) were both closed without merging, reflecting caution around a regression in Nix 2.34.0 that broke authentication for private caches.
- [#2045](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2045) highlights ongoing **OxCaml compatibility tracking** as a recurring concern, with the OxCaml fork of Merlin surfacing edge cases in parser error recovery not yet seen in the upstream build.
repositories.contributors_summary: Contributions this week were limited to issue triage by voodoos and Liam Stevenson, and an automated PR by Dependabot.
repositories.contributors:
- [@voodoos](https://github.com/voodoos) triaged [#2045](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2045), identified it as OxCaml-specific, and routed it to the appropriate team.
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) investigated [#2045](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2045) and identified `debug-on-error` as the immediate cause.
- [Ben Lieberman](https://github.com/bhlieberman) filed [#2045](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2045) with a detailed stack trace and confirmed the `debug-on-error` finding.
- Dependabot opened [#2046](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2046) to update the Nix CI action dependency.

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with sandboxing enabled by default in 3.23, async digests merged, regression fix for dune lang 1.0 select semantics, and the version bumped to 3.23.
repositories.new_features_summary: Sandboxing is now enabled by default for user rules from dune lang 3.23, and target digests are computed asynchronously for improved performance.
repositories.new_features:
- **Sandboxing on by default** for user rules starting from dune lang 3.23, helping catch undeclared dependencies ([#13805](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13805))
- **Asynchronous digests** (part 2): target files and directories are now digested in background threads, reducing blocking during builds ([#13341](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13341))
- **Jsoo config management** moved out of dune, reducing the need for dune to know all possible js_of_ocaml configs upfront ([#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613))
- **Slang simplifications** for package management: deduplicate `And`/`Or` blangs, simplify `when` with `Nil` body, and substitute `installed` variable at solve time for absent packages ([#13772](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13772), [#13773](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13773), [#13776](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13776))
repositories.activity_summary: The week saw 46 commits with significant internal refactoring, several bug fixes including a dune lang 1.0 regression, and the project bumped to version 3.23.
repositories.activity:
- Newly opened PR [#13813](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13813) proposes allowing sandboxed actions to produce corrected files without declaring targets
- Newly opened PR [#13800](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13800) proposes a `@revdep` alias to build all reverse dependencies of libraries in a directory
- Newly opened PR [#13792](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13792) resolves directory symlinks in fetched package targets, a step toward the relocatable compiler work
- Newly opened PR [#13791](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13791) adds clarifying documentation to `Io.links` to fix confusing `src`/`dst` semantics
- Newly opened PR [#13749](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13749) adds a how-to guide for using multiple dune contexts (e.g. ThreadSanitizer)
- Fixed **regression in dune lang 1.0** `(select ..)` semantics for non-OCaml files, which was causing build failures for packages like `mccs` ([#13804](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13804))
- Fixed **silent hang** when dune tries to connect to a non-existent RPC server while the build directory is locked ([#13767](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13767))
- Fixed **trace file placement**: default trace file is now placed inside the build directory at workspace root rather than relative to the current directory ([#13735](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13735))
- Fixed **Rocq module duplication** error: internal error when a module is shared between `rocq.theory` and `rocq.extraction` stanzas now produces a hint ([#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13733))
- Fixed **opam test target generation** for packages using `(dir ..)`, limiting the target to the package directory ([#13778](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13778))
- Version bumped to **3.23** on the main branch ([#13777](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13777))
- Extensive internal refactoring of digest, fs_cache, and rule cache modules in preparation for async digest work
- Test reorganization: fs memo tests split by category, install command tests moved to own directory
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussion around the 3.22.0 release, Dune RPC API compatibility with ocaml-lsp-server, and usability of dune contexts.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The **3.22.0 release tracker** ([#13674](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13674)) saw discussion about a `dune-rpc` API change that breaks `ocaml-lsp-server`; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) confirmed the breakage was intentional and plans to fix lsp compatibility separately
- **Usability issues with dune contexts** ([#13768](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13768)) were reported, including `dune exec --context` not picking up the right binary and lack of flags to target specific contexts in `dune build`
- **RPC/watch mode**: a new issue ([#13788](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13788)) reports that a short `dune build @@fmt` command blocks until the watch process finishes its current targets, making editor on-save hooks impractical
- Discussion on whether caching should be enabled for OCaml compilation and linking by default ([#13759](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13759))
repositories.trends_summary: Continued investment in correctness (sandboxing by default, async digests) and package management improvements for the relocatable compiler roadmap.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Sandboxing by default** in dune 3.23 signals a push toward stricter build correctness guarantees for user rules
- **Async digest pipeline** progressing through multiple PRs to move blocking I/O off the main build loop
- **Relocatable compiler** work continues, with `installed` variable substitution at solve time and ongoing symlink resolution in fetched targets
- **Slang expression simplification** series improving opam variable translation correctness and efficiency at lock time
- **Jsoo build improvements** active across multiple PRs aiming at better incrementality and config separation

repositories.brief_summary: Four PRs merged this week, fixing bugs in the markdown backend, URL remapping, type substitution, and incremental builds.
repositories.activity_summary: The week saw four PRs merged, covering bugfixes across the markdown backend, URL remapping, type substitution crash, and incremental build handling.
repositories.activity:
- [#1403](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1403) fixed the **markdown backend** to emit `ocaml` as the language identifier for OCaml API declarations, and refactored `documentedSrc` to collect all code items into a unified block with inline doc comments.
- [#1400](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1400) resolved long-standing issue [#930](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/930), a **crash in type substitution** triggered when substituting the same name at different arities, by fixing the arity mismatch in `subst.ml` and adding dedicated edge-case tests.
- [#1395](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1395) fixed **URL remapping for page references**, where the prefix check was incorrectly applied only to the directory path, causing page links to go unremapped; the fix includes the filename in the prefix match and normalises remap entries at config creation time.
- [#1402](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1402) applied two further bugfixes important for upcoming dune rules, ensuring **incremental build** correctness and that `--warn-error` consistently promotes all warnings to errors.
- [#1393](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1393) bumped the **`actions/checkout` GitHub Action** from version 5 to 6.
repositories.trends_summary: Work this week concentrated on correctness fixes across the markdown generator and the xref2 resolution engine, with test coverage expanded for several edge cases.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **markdown backend** ([#1403](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1403)) received iterative refinement after its initial introduction, with [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx) landing improvements to language tagging and code-block consolidation.
- The **xref2 substitution engine** continues to receive targeted correctness fixes; [#1400](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1400) added a dedicated test directory `test/xref2/github_issue_930.t` to guard against regression of the arity-mismatch crash.
- The **`--warn-error` flag** behaviour was tightened in [#1402](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1402) to cover unresolved-reference warnings that were previously unaffected.
repositories.contributors_summary: Three contributors had activity merged this week, with Jon Ludlam accounting for the majority of the merged work.
repositories.contributors:
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) merged [#1402](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1402), [#1400](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1400), and [#1395](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1395), covering incremental-build fixes, the type-substitution crash, and URL remapping.
- [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx) contributed [#1403](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1403), improving OCaml language tagging and code-block rendering in the markdown backend.
- [@dependabot](https://github.com/dependabot) opened and had merged [#1393](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1393) to update the `actions/checkout` action to v6.

repositories.brief_summary: Tutorial improvements for devcontainer onboarding, scraper CI optimisation, exercise sort fix, and a wave of new OCaml Planet posts.
repositories.new_features_summary: Tutorials now reference the OCaml devcontainer as a zero-install path, and scrape workflows use a pre-built binary release.
repositories.new_features:
- The **Installing OCaml** and **Configuring Your Editor** tutorials were updated by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) ([#3558](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3558)) to mention the [OCaml devcontainer](https://github.com/tarides/ocaml-devcontainer) as an option for skipping local installation, with notes that it ships with OCaml LSP, OCamlFormat, and editor support pre-configured.
- A new **Dev Containers** section was added to the Docker Images tutorial ([#3558](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3558)) to distinguish the development-oriented devcontainer setup from CI-focused Docker images.
- A new GitHub Actions workflow `release-scrapers.yml` was introduced by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) ([#3556](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3556)) to build and publish the scraper binary as a `scraper-latest` release, eliminating the need to recompile from source on every scrape run.
- The **scrape workflows** were updated ([#3557](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3557)) to download the pre-built `scrape.exe` binary instead of rebuilding it, installing only runtime libraries (`libev4`, `libonig5`) rather than the full OCaml toolchain.

repositories.brief_summary: New --build-config CLI flags, OCaml 5.5 alpha support, shape computation performance fix, and runtime fixes merged.
repositories.new_features_summary: New --build-config/--apply-build-config CLI flags and OCaml 5.5.0~alpha1 compiler and runtime support added.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`--build-config`** and **`--apply-build-config`** CLI flags added to both `js_of_ocaml` and `wasm_of_ocaml` compilers by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177)). `--build-config` prints build-relevant compiler settings as key=value pairs and exits; `--apply-build-config` overrides these settings, taking precedence over other flags. This enables coherent compilation when linking units that must share the same settings.
- **OCaml 5.5.0~alpha1 support** added by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo), including new JS and Wasm runtime primitives for `gc`, `sys`, and `domain` modules, plus a fix for int division return type in the Wasm compiler ([#2197](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2197)).
- **Shape computation performance** overhauled by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) to fix potential exponential blowups: coinductive computation with caching in `Flow.the_shape_of`, sharing in `Shape.to_string`, and more precise function arity computation. [Ricky Vetter](https://github.com/rickyvetter) reported a case that went from over 7 hours to ~200ms compilation time ([#2198](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2198)).
- **Binaryen version check** added to the `wasm_of_ocaml` runtime build to verify that Binaryen >= 119 is available, providing a clear error message when the `wasm-merge` tool is missing ([#2191](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2191)). This resolves the build failure reported in [#2190](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2190).
- **Runtime fixes** including various corrections to the Wasm and JS runtimes ([#2192](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2192)) and additional **bug fixes** in the compiler ([#2194](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2194)), both by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon).
repositories.activity_summary: Ongoing Wasm backend work continues on dynlink/toplevel support, WASI runtime, and ECMAScript modules; OxCaml support PR remains under review.
repositories.activity:
- **Ongoing: Dynlink and toplevel support for Wasm** ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) is open and under review, with [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) providing detailed feedback on the read-only embedded filesystem behavior (`chdir`, `mkdir`, `stat`) and code sharing opportunities.
- **Ongoing: WASI runtime** ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) remains blocked on [#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177) (now merged), [ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613), and [#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189).
- **Draft: OCaml 5.5 full support** ([#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195)) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) is a large draft PR (12k additions) covering comprehensive Wasm and JS changes for OCaml 5.5, with a known Wasm int test failure under investigation by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon).
- **Ongoing: OxCaml support** ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) is under continued review by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo), covering `or_null` type support via JavaScript `null` and ppxlib integration.
- **Draft: ECMAScript modules tooling** ([#2163](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2163)) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) remains in draft with initial compiler tooling for ESM output.
- **Draft: Compiler hints** work by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) spans two related draft PRs: consuming OCaml physical equality hints ([#1745](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1745)) and hints produced by [ocaml/ocaml#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438) ([#1721](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1721)).
- **`onbeforeunload` handler fix** ([#2159](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2159)) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) fixing [#1436](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1436) remains open, adding `Dom.beforeUnloadEvent` and `Dom_html.beforeUnloadEvent` class types and correcting the boolean return value handling for modern browsers.
- **Issue closed**: [Cedric Cellier](https://github.com/rixed) reported a build failure with `wasm-merge: not found` when installing `wasm_of_ocaml-compiler` 6.3.2 ([#2190](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2190)), resolved by the Binaryen version check added in [#2191](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2191).
repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussions on the --build-config PR and shape computation PR highlighted coordination challenges with force-pushes and benchmark regressions.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The **`--build-config` PR** ([#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177)) involved back-and-forth between [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) and [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) after `git push --force-with-lease` unexpectedly dropped commits that [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) had pushed to the shared branch, highlighting the risk of force-pushing to collaborative branches.
- The **shape computation PR** ([#2198](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2198)) sparked discussion about a 15% increase in compilation time for `ocamlc`, with [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) adding an extra optimization round to compensate. [Ricky Vetter](https://github.com/rickyvetter) provided a real-world benchmark showing a reduction from >7 hours to ~200ms for pathological cases.
repositories.trends_summary: Sustained focus on Wasm backend maturation: WASI, dynlink, and OCaml 5.5 compatibility work progressing in parallel.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Wasm backend feature parity** is a sustained focus, with multiple concurrent PRs ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187), [#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831), [#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195)) advancing dynlink/toplevel support, WASI runtime, and OCaml 5.5 compatibility for the Wasm backend.
- **Compiler build configuration** is being formalized: the new `--build-config`/`--apply-build-config` flags ([#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177)) and the `build_config.mli` / `build_info.mli` API refactoring indicate work toward reproducible, coherent multi-unit compilation.
- **Compiler optimization** continues with the shape computation overhaul ([#2198](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2198)) and the pending variable coalescing PR ([#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166)), both aimed at reducing generated code size and V8 stack frame usage.
repositories.contributors_summary: Jérôme Vouillon and @hhugo drove all code contributions this week, with Ricky Vetter providing key benchmark feedback.
repositories.contributors:
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) merged five PRs this week: the `--build-config`/`--apply-build-config` CLI flags ([#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177)), shape computation performance improvements ([#2198](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2198)), runtime fixes ([#2192](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2192)), bug fixes ([#2194](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2194)), and the Binaryen version check ([#2191](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2191)). He also engaged in active review discussions on the dynlink/toplevel ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)), OCaml 5.5 ([#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195)), and OxCaml ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)) PRs.
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) merged three PRs: OCaml 5.5.0~alpha1 support ([#2197](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2197)), JS parser test fixes ([#2199](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2199)), and the biome upgrade ([#2196](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2196)). He provided detailed code review on the `--build-config` PR ([#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177)), pushing refactoring commits and raising design questions about the `toplevel` flag handling, and reviewed the dynlink/toplevel PR ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)) with feedback on filesystem semantics.
- [Ricky Vetter](https://github.com/rickyvetter) provided a benchmark report on the shape computation PR ([#2198](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2198)), confirming a pathological case dropped from >7 hours to ~200ms compilation time.
- [Cedric Cellier](https://github.com/rixed) reported the `wasm-merge: not found` build failure ([#2190](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2190)) on Debian bookworm with js_of_ocaml 6.3.2, which was subsequently resolved.

repositories.brief_summary: A highly active week spanning type system advances, reaper stabilization, backend optimizations, and continued OCaml 5.4 merge work.
repositories.new_features_summary: Key additions include a new -O4 optimization level that bundles reaper with -O3, the [@unpacked] attribute for unboxed product arguments in C stubs, merged equivalent CFG blocks pass, and extended constant propagation to predecessor terminators and float/integer arithmetic.
repositories.new_features:
- A new **`-O4` optimization level** was introduced ([#5574](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5574)), which activates `-O3` together with the reaper dead-code elimination pass, giving users a convenient combined handle; the `[@flambda_o4]` attribute mirrors it at the per-function level.
- The **`[@unpacked]` attribute** for C stub externals ([#5610](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5610)) allows unboxed product types as C stub arguments by flattening each product component into separate native C arguments, while the bytecode stub still receives a single boxed value for compatibility.
- A **CFG block merging pass** (`-cfg-merge-blocks`) was merged ([#5597](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5597)), deduplicating equivalent basic blocks after register allocation and achieving up to 4.5% reduction in instruction count with only 0.2% build-time overhead.
- **Constant propagation** in the CFG backend was extended to cover integer arithmetic ([#5545](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5545)), floating-point arithmetic ([#5546](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5546)), and inferred values from predecessor terminators ([#5562](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5562)), enabling more aggressive CFG terminator simplification.
- The **`Multicore.spawn_result` type** now accepts `value_or_null` resources ([#5579](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5579)), aligning the multicore API with the broader null-safety story in OxCaml.
- **`with kind_` constraints** were added as part of abstract kinds part 3 ([#5629](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5629)), completing the core feature set for abstract kinds ahead of a broader announcement.
- A new **`Ptyp_newlayout` AST node** and `layout_` syntax for locally abstract layouts was introduced in the parsetree, supporting layout-polymorphic type checking work.
- **`ocamldep` now scans `with kind` annotations** ([#5628](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5628)), ensuring correct dependency tracking for modules using abstract kinds.
repositories.activity_summary: Twenty-eight PRs were merged this week covering typing improvements, reaper stabilization, 5.4 upstream merge progress, and backend infrastructure work.
repositories.activity:
- [#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608) adds **`write` and `observable` modes** to the mode system, introducing a partial order diamond where `read` and `write` are incomparable, with a bit-vector encoding in the solver for performance; this is under active review awaiting feedback from layout-poly maintainers.
- [#5627](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5627) proposes **optimized Patricia trees** that bitpack two ints together and specialize common operations, delivering 3–11% compile-time speedups and up to 19% reduced allocations on key compiler files.
- [#5629](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5629) opens **abstract kinds part 3** with `with kind_` constraints, the final planned feature before announcing abstract kinds publicly, including full cycle checking to prevent recursive kinds.
- [#5630](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5630) **hoists primitive repr tables** out of `prim_has_valid_reprs`, avoiding repeated reconstruction of large `String.Map` values and achieving up to 1.79x faster compilation of modules with many external declarations.
- [#5623](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5623) adds a **CI workflow for continuation specialization**, expanding test coverage for the match-in-match optimization.
- [#5615](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5615) removes the **`~level` parameter** from jkind comparison/violation APIs by introducing a `level_fresh` sentinel, significantly simplifying call sites across 14 files.
- [#5601](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5601) extends **with-bounds to support external modalities**, allowing types like `type 'a t : value mod portable external_ with 'a @@ external_`.
- [#5602](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5602) adds **syntax for mode constraints without a type** — `(expr : @ modes)` — and updates `pprintast` to use the new syntax, with user-facing documentation updated.
- [#5569](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5569) works to **remove legacy mode syntax from the AST**, making `pprintast` emit new-style mode annotations and addressing roundtrip issues with `local_` desugaring.
- [#5577](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5577) passes **magic number versions via `./configure`**, simplifying the process of minting new tags without needing a separate magic number bump.
- [#5628](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5628) was **merged** so `ocamldep` now scans `with kind` annotations for dependency tracking.
- [#5625](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5625) **reverted** the unboxed GADT jkind projection fix ([#5580](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5580)) after an internal build failure, and [#5580](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5580) was subsequently re-merged after verifying the internal codebase.
- [#5624](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5624) bumped **magic numbers for 5.2.0minus-34**.
- [#5517](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5517) merged **abstract kinds part 2.5** adding kind declarations in recursive modules with cycle detection.
- [#5498](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5498) merged the **canonical layout poly value description**, implementing parsing, printing, and inclusion checks for layout-polymorphic value signatures.
- [#5575](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5575) fixed **buggy abstract kinds error messages** including a `fatal_error` triggered during error printing.
- Multiple **reaper** fixes were merged: [#5593](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5593) adds a zero_alloc hack for local fields, [#5611](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5611) improves error messages, [#5612](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5612) handles unboxed arguments with no sources, [#5619](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5619) enables local fields under -O4, [#5595](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5595) wraps `Not_found` with proper fatal errors, and [#5590](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5590) stops exporting toplevel functions in cmx.
- [#5588](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5588) added a **reaper + local value slots CI job** to expand coverage.
- [#5609](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5609) improved the **gather regalloc fix** by marking index operand as overwritten for more robust register allocation with SIMD gather instructions.
- [#5589](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5589) assures **gather operands are distinct** by refreshing the index register during instruction selection.
- [#5620](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5620) merged a **workaround for Sort genvar assertion failures** as a stopgap while the proper fix ([#5615](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5615)) is prepared.
- [#5606](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5606) reverted inclusion error messages back to **"the first"/"the second"** phrasing for better alignment with upstream OCaml.
- Several **5.4 merge** PRs were merged: [#5618](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5618) for boot-compiler build fixes, [#5599](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5599) for printer conflicts, [#5582](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5582) for env operation renames, [#5504](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5504) for lambda conflicts, and [#5585](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5585) for root file fixes.
- [#5610](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5610) was **merged**, adding `[@unpacked]` for unboxed product C stub arguments.
- [#5592](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5592) fixed a **typo in `value_kind`** nullability scraping, correcting subtle level mutation bugs.
- [#5576](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5576) refactored the reaper by **extracting generic datalog helpers** into a separate module.
- [#5452](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5452) replaced **polymorphic morphism comparisons** with explicit equality functions.
- [#5002](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5002) added **equality functions for CFG basic and terminator instructions** as infrastructure for the block merging pass.
repositories.discussions_summary: Significant discussions this week centered on mode system design trade-offs, memprof parallel safety, and 5.4 merge strategy decisions.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608) sparked discussion between [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) and [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) on whether the `write`/`observable` diamond should use separate non-diamond axes internally for performance; [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) demonstrated that a bit-vector encoding makes the diamond approach equally efficient.
- [#5600](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5600) on parallel **Memprof bugs** attracted detailed review from [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) who identified an edge case where a near-zero-lambda profile started after stopping could trigger assertion failures, and proposed precise fixes for the config invalidation race.
- [#5544](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5544) on **slambda fracturing** received extensive review from [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer) working through each case of the fracture pass, with [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) iterating on the design.
- [#5597](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5597) on **CFG block merging** included discussion with [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) about hash function correctness and whether mutable fields (like `terminator`) cause problems for block equality hashing.
repositories.trends_summary: The week shows sustained investment in three parallel tracks: mode system expressiveness, reaper dead-code elimination maturation, and the large OCaml 5.4 upstream merge effort.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **reaper pass** is rapidly stabilizing, with multiple bugfixes landing around local fields, zero_alloc interactions, and better error messages, suggesting it is approaching production readiness at `-O4`.
- **Abstract kinds** are nearing feature completeness with recursive module support and `with kind_` constraints now implemented, setting up for a broader announcement.
- The **mode system** continues to expand in expressiveness, with `write`/`observable` modes, external modality in with-bounds, and improved syntax all progressing in parallel.
- **Backend infrastructure** around CFG equality and constant propagation is being built out systematically by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc), enabling increasingly sophisticated optimizations like block merging.
- The **OCaml 5.4 merge** effort continues across multiple parallel PRs covering runtime, stdlib, lambda, parsing, and typing subsystems.
repositories.issues_summary: One issue was filed and closed this week, regarding a build failure when installing utop via the OxCaml installation guide.
repositories.good_first_issues: - [#5603](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5603) — a **utop installation failure** on macOS arm64 due to `lwt 6.1.1` incompatibility with the OxCaml type system was reported by a new user and closed with a workaround: pin `lwt 6.0.0+ox` before installing utop.

repositories.brief_summary: Open PR adding missing_ok to Eio.Path.unlink and active discussion on tempdir utilities proposal.
repositories.activity_summary: PR #833 adding missing_ok to Eio.Path.unlink remains open and mergeable; tempdir proposal #831 received design feedback.
repositories.activity:
- Open PR by [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) adding a **`missing_ok` parameter to `Eio.Path.unlink`** to silently ignore missing files, referenced as addressing [#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828) — the change is small (8 additions, 3 deletions across 2 files) and marked mergeable ([#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833))
- **Tempdir utilities proposal** by [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) requesting `Eio.Stdenv.tmp`, `Eio.Path.open_temp_dir`, and `Eio.Path.with_temp_dir` remains open for design discussion ([#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831))
repositories.discussions_summary: Design discussion on tempdir API shape including backend portability and optional cleanup behaviour.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) responded to the **tempdir proposal** ([#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831)) noting that `Eio` should not depend on `eio.unix`, so `run_in_systhread` cannot be used directly; he suggested routing the temp directory through `Stdenv` for backend portability (e.g. `/tmp`, `$TMPDIR`, Windows `get_temp_path`), and raised the question of whether cleanup should be optional via an argument rather than always automatic
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) also approved PR [#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833) adding `missing_ok` to `Eio.Path.unlink`, commenting "this looks good to me"
repositories.emerging_trends: - [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) is contributing a cluster of **filesystem convenience utilities** — `missing_ok` for unlink ([#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833)) and tempdir management ([#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831)) — filling gaps that users commonly work around with `Eio.Stdenv.fs` and `Filename` stdlib functions
repositories.contributors_summary: Volker Diels-Grabsch authored open PR and proposal; Patrick Ferris provided review and design feedback.
repositories.contributors:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) authored the open PR adding `missing_ok` to `Eio.Path.unlink` ([#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833)) and the tempdir utilities proposal ([#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831))
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) reviewed and approved PR [#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833) and provided detailed design feedback on the tempdir proposal ([#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831)) covering API shape, backend portability, and cleanup semantics

group_summaries.brief_summary: ~30 opam packages merged including new linter and SIMD tensor backend; ocaml.org improved devcontainer tutorials and scraper CI.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New packages: OCaml linter (olint), installer builder (oui), libinput bindings, effects-based parser (parseff); devcontainer tutorial integration on ocaml.org.
group_summaries.new_features:
**ocaml/opam-repository**
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29543](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29543) **olint.0.1.0** — new OCaml linter inspired by Rust's Clippy, checking 10 antipattern rules using compiler-libs on the untyped AST, by [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham)
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29523](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29523) **oui.0.1.0** — a tool that builds standalone installers for OCaml applications, by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb)
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29531](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29531) **libinput.0.1** — initial release of OCaml bindings for the libinput input device library, by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29526](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29526) **parseff.0.1.0** — direct-style parser combinator library for OCaml 5 powered by algebraic effects, by [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx) (open, CI issue)
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29541](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29541) **dnsrobot.0.1.0** — OCaml client for DNS Robot with 11 API methods using cohttp-lwt-unix (open, needs reporter action)
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29536](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29536) **dune-ai-context.0.1.2** — tool to extract dune external dependencies and generate interface context for AI coding tools

**ocaml/ocaml.org**
- The **Installing OCaml** and **Configuring Your Editor** tutorials were updated by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3558](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3558)) to mention the OCaml devcontainer as a zero-install path, with notes that it ships with OCaml LSP, OCamlFormat, and editor support pre-configured.
- A new **Dev Containers** section was added to the Docker Images tutorial ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3558](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3558)) to distinguish development-oriented devcontainer setup from CI-focused Docker images.
- A new GitHub Actions workflow `release-scrapers.yml` was introduced by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3556](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3556)) to build and publish the scraper binary as a `scraper-latest` release, eliminating the need to recompile from source on every scrape run.
- The **scrape workflows** were updated ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3557](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3557)) to download the pre-built `scrape.exe` binary instead of rebuilding it, installing only runtime libraries (`libev4`, `libonig5`) rather than the full OCaml toolchain.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) saw approximately 30 merged PRs, covering new packages, security library updates, infrastructure tooling, and a notable policy change on package immutability enforcement
- [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) landed tutorial improvements referencing the OCaml devcontainer, scraper CI optimisation using a pre-built binary release, an exercise sort bug fix, and a wave of new OCaml Planet posts
- Key security-related updates included [ocaml/opam-repository#29532](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29532) tls 2.0.4 (exception fix) and [ocaml/opam-repository#29545](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29545) mirage-crypto 2.1.0 (NIST curve primitive exposure)
- Infrastructure changes in both repos reflect a shared push toward reproducibility: immutability enforcement in opam-repository and pre-built CI binaries in ocaml.org
- Over 20 contributors were active across both repositories this week

group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **ppxlib** spans both repos: [ocaml/opam-repository#29507](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29507) published `ppxlib.0.38.0~5.5preview` for the upcoming OCaml 5.5 release, while [ocaml/ocaml.org#3560](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3560) cleaned up the ppxlib developer meeting entry from governance data, reflecting the ongoing evolution of that project
- **OxCaml SIMD content** appeared in both repos: [ocaml/opam-repository#29490](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29490) raven.1.0.0~alpha3 introduced an nx-oxcaml SIMD tensor backend, while the OCaml Planet feed on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) ingested Mark Elvers' posts on an OxCaml SIMD ONNX inference engine and Pi Day benchmarks — both reflect the same underlying OxCaml capability
- **Dune ecosystem tooling** featured in opam-repository spans concerns relevant to ocaml.org's package documentation: [ocaml/opam-repository#29535](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29535) dune 3.22.0~alpha1 and the associated constraint PR [ocaml/opam-repository#29544](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29544) will affect how packages are built across the ecosystem once released
- **Relocatability infrastructure** merged at [ocaml/opam-repository#29451](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29451) introduces a `relocatable.packages` virtual package coordinated with upstream dune package management work, which will eventually affect how packages are installed and documented across the ecosystem
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29072](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29072) **Stricter immutability policy** merged after months of discussion — strengthens the rule that published package tarballs must not change, now a hard enforcement in opam-repo-ci lint; this directly triggered the tm-grammars 1.0.0 checksum situation and the decision to release 1.0.1 instead of patching, by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm)
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29451](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29451) **relocatable.packages virtual package** merged — introduces a virtual package that non-relocatable packages (compilers < 5.5, ocamlfind, ocamlbuild) can conflict with, enabling Dune package management to require relocatable installs; included discussion of naming conventions for virtual/property packages, by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29530](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29530) **lwt 6 constraint refinements** — open PR adjusting upper bounds across ~84 files after lwt 6 removed long-deprecated APIs (`Lwt_main.exit_hooks`, `Lwt_unix.run`, abstract `Lwt_engine`); [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) noted 440 downstream failures with lwt.6.1.1, by [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust)
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29535](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29535) **dune 3.22.0~alpha1** draft — extensive alpha release with 30+ bug fixes; a breaking API change in dune-rpc was identified and addressed in a separate constraint PR [ocaml/opam-repository#29544](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29544), with a regression in mccs builds also filed upstream, by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder)
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OCaml 5 effects adoption**: [ocaml/opam-repository#29526](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29526) parseff uses algebraic effects for parser backtracking, and [ocaml/opam-repository#29490](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29490) raven's nx-oxcaml uses OxCaml unboxed types and SIMD intrinsics for a pure-OCaml tensor backend
- **Tooling ecosystem growth**: new packages for code review management (crs), dune file linting (dunolint), OCaml source linting ([ocaml/opam-repository#29543](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29543) olint), and AI coding assistance ([ocaml/opam-repository#29536](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29536) dune-ai-context) all appeared this week
- **Reproducibility infrastructure tightening**: the merged immutability policy ([ocaml/opam-repository#29072](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29072)) and the switch to a pre-built scraper binary ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3556](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3556), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3557](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3557)) both reflect a push toward deterministic, reproducible workflows across the ecosystem
- **Relocatability groundwork**: the `relocatable.packages` virtual package ([ocaml/opam-repository#29451](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29451)) sets up infrastructure for Dune's package management mode to enforce switch portability on OCaml 5.5+
- **MirageOS and unikernels in aerospace**: [Thomas Gazagnaire](https://github.com/samoht)'s Planet posts ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3561](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3561)) connect MirageOS and unikernel concepts to satellite software, NASA's F Prime framework, and multi-tenant payload isolation, suggesting an emerging thread at the intersection of OCaml systems research and space computing

group_summaries.brief_summary: Dune 3.23 lands sandboxing by default and async digests; js_of_ocaml gains OCaml 5.5 alpha support and shape computation speedup.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune 3.23 enables sandboxing by default; js_of_ocaml adds --build-config flags, OCaml 5.5 alpha support, and shape computation overhaul.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Sandboxing enabled by default for user rules from dune lang 3.23, catching undeclared dependencies ([ocaml/dune#13805](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13805))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Asynchronous digest computation (part 2) — target files and directories now digested in background threads, reducing main build loop blocking ([ocaml/dune#13341](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13341))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Jsoo config management moved out of dune, reducing the need for dune to track all possible js_of_ocaml configs upfront ([ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Slang simplifications for package management — deduplicate `And`/`Or` blangs, simplify `when` with `Nil` body, and substitute `installed` variable at solve time for absent packages ([ocaml/dune#13772](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13772), [ocaml/dune#13773](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13773), [ocaml/dune#13776](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13776))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: New `--build-config` and `--apply-build-config` CLI flags for both `js_of_ocaml` and `wasm_of_ocaml`, enabling coherent multi-unit compilation by printing and overriding build-relevant compiler settings ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: OCaml 5.5.0~alpha1 support added, including new JS and Wasm runtime primitives for `gc`, `sys`, and `domain` modules ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2197](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2197))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: Shape computation performance overhauled with coinductive caching in `Flow.the_shape_of`, fixing potential exponential blowups — a pathological case went from >7 hours to ~200ms compilation time ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2198](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2198))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: Binaryen version check added to the `wasm_of_ocaml` runtime build to verify Binaryen >= 119 is available, providing a clear error on missing `wasm-merge` ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2191](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2191))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) was the most active repository this week with 46 commits, bumping to version 3.23 with sandboxing on by default and async digests merged
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) saw substantial activity with 8+ PRs merged, covering OCaml 5.5 alpha support, new CLI build-config flags, and a major shape computation performance fix
- [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) had 4 PRs merged addressing bugs in the markdown backend, URL remapping, type substitution, and incremental builds ([ocaml/odoc#1403](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1403), [ocaml/odoc#1400](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1400), [ocaml/odoc#1395](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1395), [ocaml/odoc#1402](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1402))
- [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) had three PRs under active review covering Windows portability ([ocaml/opam#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862)), Darcs VCS cleanup ([ocaml/opam#6866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6866)), and shell PATH ordering ([ocaml/opam#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859))
- [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) had minimal activity: two Dependabot CI PRs closed without merging and one OxCaml compatibility issue triaged ([ocaml/merlin#2045](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2045))
- [ocaml/opam-file-format](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format) had one README update PR opened ([ocaml/opam-file-format#65](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format/issues/65)); [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) had one Dune 3.22 compatibility fix opened ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1594))
- [ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind) and [ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn) had no activity this week
group_summaries.activity_summary: Bug fixes across odoc, dune, and opam; ongoing Wasm backend maturation in js_of_ocaml with dynlink, WASI, and OCaml 5.5 PRs in review.
group_summaries.activity:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Fixed regression in dune lang 1.0 `(select ..)` semantics for non-OCaml files (affecting packages like `mccs`) ([ocaml/dune#13804](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13804))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Fixed silent hang when dune connects to a non-existent RPC server while the build directory is locked ([ocaml/dune#13767](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13767))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Fixed default trace file placement to use build directory at workspace root ([ocaml/dune#13735](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13735))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Fixed Rocq module duplication internal error with a user-facing hint ([ocaml/dune#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13733))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Opened PRs for symlink resolution in fetched package targets ([ocaml/dune#13792](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13792)), `@revdep` alias for reverse dependency builds ([ocaml/dune#13800](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13800)), and a how-to guide for multiple dune contexts ([ocaml/dune#13749](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13749))

- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)**: Markdown backend now emits `ocaml` as the language identifier for OCaml API declarations; `documentedSrc` refactored to consolidate code items into a unified block ([ocaml/odoc#1403](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1403))
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)**: Windows `_WIN32_WINNT` fix for MSYS2 environments updated to place the flag in the dune build file rather than C source ([ocaml/opam#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: Dynlink and toplevel support for Wasm under review ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)); WASI runtime PR ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)) unblocked by the merged jsoo config management changes
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: Large draft PR for full OCaml 5.5 support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195)) under investigation for a Wasm int test failure
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: Build failure with `wasm-merge: not found` on Debian bookworm ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2190](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2190)) resolved by the Binaryen version check ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2191](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2191))
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp)**: Dune 3.22 compilation compatibility fix opened ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1594)); Nix flake lock updated to 2026-03-14 snapshot ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437))
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)**: Two Dependabot bumps for `cachix/install-nix-action` ([ocaml/merlin#2035](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2035), [ocaml/merlin#2046](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2046)) closed without merging due to a regression in Nix 2.34.0
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Dune → ocaml-lsp breakage**: The dune 3.22 `dune-rpc` API change broke `ocaml-lsp-server` (discussed in [ocaml/dune#13674](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13674)); [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) opened [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1594) to fix ocaml-lsp's build, and [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) noted lsp compatibility will be fixed separately
