- **[Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) active across opam and opam-file-format**: Filed [ocaml/opam#6863](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6863), reviewed [ocaml/opam#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859), and opened [ocaml/opam-file-format#65](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format/issues/65) this week
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Dune RPC API compatibility** ([ocaml/dune#13674](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13674)): the 3.22.0 release tracker saw discussion about an intentional `dune-rpc` API change that breaks `ocaml-lsp-server`; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) confirmed the break was deliberate and plans to fix LSP compatibility separately
- **Dune RPC/watch mode blocking** ([ocaml/dune#13788](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13788)): a new issue reports that `dune build @@fmt` blocks until the watch process completes its current targets, making editor on-save hooks impractical
- **Dune context usability** ([ocaml/dune#13768](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13768)): reported that `dune exec --context` does not pick up the right binary and that there is no per-context flag for `dune build`, complicating multi-compiler setups
- **Merlin OxCaml Emacs debugger issue** ([ocaml/merlin#2045](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2045)): [Ben Lieberman](https://github.com/bhlieberman) reported the Lisp debugger firing repeatedly in the OxCaml variant of Merlin (5.2.1-502+ox); [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) triaged it as OxCaml-specific, and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) identified `debug-on-error` as the immediate trigger though the underlying parser recovery exception remains open
- **js_of_ocaml shape computation performance** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2198](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2198)): discussion around a 15% increase in `ocamlc` compilation time from the fix, with [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) adding an extra optimization round; [Ricky Vetter](https://github.com/rickyvetter) confirmed a real-world pathological case dropped from >7 hours to ~200ms
- **js_of_ocaml force-push incident** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177)): a `git push --force-with-lease` unexpectedly dropped commits pushed by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) to a shared branch, highlighting risks of force-pushing to collaborative branches
- **opam Darcs VCS model** ([ocaml/opam#6866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6866)): [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) provided detailed commentary on how the current `module VCS` abstraction is Git-centric and does not fit Darcs's context-file approach and weak hashes; [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) welcomed follow-up PRs
- **opam shell hook PATH ordering** ([ocaml/opam#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859)): [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) flagged that in-place PATH updates could be a breaking change for users who add paths after sourcing `opam-init.sh` and recommended a prominent release note
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Sandboxing and build correctness**: dune 3.23's sandboxing-by-default ([ocaml/dune#13805](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13805)) continues the trend toward stricter build guarantees, complemented by the async digest pipeline ([ocaml/dune#13341](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13341)) moving blocking I/O off the main build loop
- **Wasm backend maturation in js_of_ocaml**: multiple concurrent PRs advancing dynlink/toplevel support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)), WASI runtime ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)), and full OCaml 5.5 compatibility ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195)) indicate sustained investment in the Wasm target
- **Compiler build configuration formalization**: js_of_ocaml's new `--build-config`/`--apply-build-config` flags ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177)) and dune's jsoo config management extraction ([ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613)) together point toward reproducible, coherent multi-unit compilation across the dune/jsoo toolchain
- **Dune version compatibility as a recurring concern**: ocaml-lsp required a compatibility fix ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1594)) for dune 3.22, and js_of_ocaml's WASI PR was blocked on a dune feature; dune API changes have downstream effects across the tools group
- **Windows and shell portability**: opam continues to see improvements for Windows MSYS2 environments ([ocaml/opam#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862)) and shell hook correctness ([ocaml/opam#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859)), with cross-references to [ocaml/ocaml#14484](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14484) suggesting coordinated Windows effort
- **OxCaml compatibility tracking**: the Merlin OxCaml parser recovery issue ([ocaml/merlin#2045](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2045)) and js_of_ocaml's OxCaml support PR ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)) both active, reflecting ongoing effort to keep the tools group compatible with the OxCaml variant
- **Automated dependency update friction**: Dependabot/bot PRs for Nix CI actions closed without merging in both merlin ([ocaml/merlin#2046](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2046)) and a long-lived flake.lock PR in ocaml-lsp ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437)) reflects a pattern of automated dependency PRs stalling due to upstream regressions

group_summaries.brief_summary: Active week in oxcaml/oxcaml: type system advances, reaper stabilization, backend optimizations, and OCaml 5.4 merge progress.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New -O4 optimization level, [@unpacked] C stub attribute, CFG block merging pass, and extended constant propagation landed.
group_summaries.new_features:
- A new **`-O4` optimization level** was introduced ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5574](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5574)), bundling the reaper dead-code elimination pass with `-O3`; the `[@flambda_o4]` attribute mirrors it at the per-function level.
- The **`[@unpacked]` attribute** for C stub externals ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5610](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5610)) allows unboxed product types as C stub arguments by flattening each product component into separate native C arguments, while bytecode stubs receive a single boxed value.
- A **CFG block merging pass** (`-cfg-merge-blocks`) was merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5597](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5597)), deduplicating equivalent basic blocks after register allocation, achieving up to 4.5% reduction in instruction count with only 0.2% build-time overhead.
- **Constant propagation** in the CFG backend was extended to cover integer arithmetic ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5545](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5545)), floating-point arithmetic ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5546](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5546)), and values inferred from predecessor terminators ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5562](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5562)).
- **`Multicore.spawn_result`** now accepts `value_or_null` resources ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5579](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5579)), aligning the multicore API with the null-safety story.
- **`with kind_` constraints** were added as part of abstract kinds part 3 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5629](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5629)), completing the core feature set for abstract kinds ahead of a broader announcement.
- A new **`Ptyp_newlayout` AST node** and `layout_` syntax for locally abstract layouts was introduced, supporting layout-polymorphic type checking work.
- **`ocamldep` now scans `with kind` annotations** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5628](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5628)), ensuring correct dependency tracking for modules using abstract kinds.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was concentrated in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml); the other repositories in the group ([oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository), [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin), [oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml), [janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository)) had no recorded activity.
- Twenty-eight PRs were merged in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml), with over twenty contributors active across typing, backend, reaper, and 5.4 merge work.
- Three parallel workstreams dominated: mode system expressiveness ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5602](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5602)), reaper dead-code elimination stabilization ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5593](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5593), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5611](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5611), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5612](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5612)), and the OCaml 5.4 upstream merge ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5618](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5618), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5599](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5599), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5582](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5582)).
- Backend infrastructure investment continued with CFG block merging ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5597](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5597)) and extended constant propagation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5545](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5545), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5546](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5546), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5562](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5562)).
group_summaries.activity_summary: Twenty-eight PRs merged in oxcaml/oxcaml covering typing, reaper stabilization, 5.4 upstream merge progress, and backend infrastructure.
group_summaries.activity:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608) proposes **`write` and `observable` modes**, introducing a partial-order diamond where `read` and `write` are incomparable; a bit-vector solver encoding makes this as efficient as simpler alternatives.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5627](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5627) proposes **optimized Patricia trees** bitpacking two ints together, delivering 3–11% compile-time speedups and up to 19% reduced allocations on key compiler files.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5630](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5630) **hoists primitive repr tables** out of `prim_has_valid_reprs`, avoiding repeated reconstruction of large `String.Map` values and achieving up to 1.79× faster compilation of modules with many external declarations.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5623](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5623) adds a **CI workflow for continuation specialization**, expanding test coverage for the match-in-match optimization.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5615](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5615) removes the **`~level` parameter** from jkind comparison/violation APIs via a `level_fresh` sentinel, simplifying call sites across 14 files.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5601](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5601) extends **with-bounds to support external modalities**, allowing types like `type 'a t : value mod portable external_ with 'a @@ external_`.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5602](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5602) adds **syntax for mode constraints without a type** — `(expr : @ modes)` — and updates `pprintast` accordingly.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5569](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5569) works to **remove legacy mode syntax from the AST**, making `pprintast` emit new-style mode annotations.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5517](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5517) merged **abstract kinds part 2.5**, adding kind declarations in recursive modules with cycle detection.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5498](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5498) merged the **canonical layout poly value description**, implementing parsing, printing, and inclusion checks for layout-polymorphic value signatures.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5575](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5575) fixed **buggy abstract kinds error messages**, including a `fatal_error` triggered during error printing.
- Multiple **reaper** fixes merged: [oxcaml/oxcaml#5593](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5593) (zero_alloc hack for local fields), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5611](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5611) (improved error messages), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5612](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5612) (unboxed arguments with no sources), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5619](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5619) (local fields under -O4), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5595](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5595) (`Not_found` fatal error wrapper), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5590](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5590) (stop exporting toplevel functions in cmx).
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5588](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5588) added a **reaper + local value slots CI job** to expand coverage.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5609](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5609) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5589](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5589) fixed **SIMD gather register allocation** by marking the index operand as overwritten and refreshing the index register during instruction selection.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5625](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5625) **reverted** then [oxcaml/oxcaml#5580](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5580) **re-merged** an unboxed GADT jkind projection fix after verifying the internal codebase.
- Several **5.4 merge** PRs landed: [oxcaml/oxcaml#5618](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5618) (boot-compiler build fixes), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5599](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5599) (printer conflicts), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5582](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5582) (env operation renames), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5504](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5504) (lambda conflicts), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5585](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5585) (root file fixes).
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5577](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5577) passes **magic number versions via `./configure`**, simplifying minting new tags.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5576](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5576) refactored the reaper by **extracting generic datalog helpers** into a separate module.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5002](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5002) added **equality functions for CFG basic and terminator instructions** as infrastructure for the block merging pass.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608): [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) and [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) debated whether the `write`/`observable` diamond should use separate non-diamond axes internally for performance; [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) demonstrated that a bit-vector encoding makes the diamond approach equally efficient.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5600](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5600): [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) identified an edge case in parallel Memprof where a near-zero-lambda profile started after stopping could trigger assertion failures, and proposed precise fixes for the config invalidation race.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5544](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5544): [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer) conducted extensive review of the slambda fracturing pass, with [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) iterating on the design.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5597](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5597): [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) raised questions about hash function correctness and whether mutable fields (like `terminator`) cause problems for block equality hashing in the CFG block merging pass.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- The **reaper pass** is stabilizing rapidly, with fixes for local fields, zero_alloc interactions, and error messages ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5593](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5593), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5611](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5611), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5619](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5619)) suggesting it is approaching production readiness at `-O4`.
- **Abstract kinds** are nearing feature completeness with recursive module support ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5517](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5517)) and `with kind_` constraints ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5629](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5629)) now implemented, setting up for a broader public announcement.
- The **mode system** continues to expand, with `write`/`observable` modes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)), external modality in with-bounds ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5601](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5601)), and improved syntax ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5602](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5602)) all progressing in parallel.
- **CFG backend infrastructure** around block equality and constant propagation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5002](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5002), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5545](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5545), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5546](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5546), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5562](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5562)) is being built out systematically by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc), enabling increasingly sophisticated optimizations.
- The **OCaml 5.4 merge** effort spans multiple parallel PRs across runtime, stdlib, lambda, parsing, and typing subsystems ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5618](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5618), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5599](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5599), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5582](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5582), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5504](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5504)).

group_summaries.brief_summary: Bytecode optimization hints land for Js_of_ocaml/Wasm_of_ocaml, faster UTF-8 normalization, typing fixes, and runtime bug fixes.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Bytecode optimization hints merged, enabling Js_of_ocaml and Wasm_of_ocaml to access Lambda-level hints from .cmo/.cma files.
group_summaries.new_features:
- [ocaml/ocaml#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438) **Bytecode optimization hints**: the bytecode compiler now preserves optimization hints from the Lambda IR (closure kinds, immutable blocks, array lengths, C call details) in a new `HINT` section in `.cmo` and `.cma` files, enabled with `-bytecode-hints`. This allows [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)'s Js_of_ocaml and Wasm_of_ocaml to access type-level information previously discarded at the bytecode stage.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14618](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14618) **Faster UTF-8 normalization** outside the ASCII range was merged, optimizing the `Misc.Stdlib` unicode normalization path. Contributed by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), reviewed by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), Daniel Bünzli, and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was concentrated in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml); [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) had no recorded activity.
- Nine PRs were merged in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml), covering a major bytecode feature ([ocaml/ocaml#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438)), a performance improvement ([ocaml/ocaml#14618](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14618)), typing fixes ([ocaml/ocaml#14626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14626), [ocaml/ocaml#14638](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14638)), a runtime bug fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14635)), and documentation/test improvements.
- Sixteen new PRs were opened across the compiler, runtime, and typing areas.
- Notable open issues include bytecode heap corruption with effects ([ocaml/ocaml#14644](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14644)), a missing GC root in `reperform` ([ocaml/ocaml#14647](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14647)), and an ARM Thumb mode instruction selection bug ([ocaml/ocaml#14646](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14646)).
group_summaries.activity_summary: Nine PRs merged covering typing fixes, runtime bug fixes, documentation updates, and the bytecode hints feature; sixteen new PRs opened.
group_summaries.activity:
- [ocaml/ocaml#14626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14626) **Warning 16 false positive** with module-dependent functions fixed by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob): the typechecker now correctly handles function types with module-dependent parameters when determining whether an optional argument is unerasable.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14638](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14638) **`is_inferred` now traverses local opens** correctly, fixing the issue reported in [ocaml/ocaml#14629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14629) where a `let open` expression prevented proper detection of optional argument elimination. Merged by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14635) **`caml_floatarray_gather` control flow bug** fixed by [Marc Lasson](https://github.com/mlasson): `Float.Array.sub`, `Float.Array.append`, and `Float.Array.concat` could return a non-empty array when the result should have been `[||]`.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14645](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14645) **Ephemeron GC corner case** documented by [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) to prevent future confusion around ephemeron marking during the major GC.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14617](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14617) The **`ocaml_deprecated_cli` alert** is now made fatal in compiler development flags by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), enforcing migration away from deprecated CLI patterns.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14640](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14640) A **surplus argument in the `Fun.protect` test** was fixed by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14648) **Modular explicits: optional arguments are elided** for functional arguments to higher-order functions in the Lambda IR, opened by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) as part of ongoing modular explicit work.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14646](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14646) **ARM Thumb mode**: [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) opened a fix to avoid using the `TBH` (Table Branch Halfword) instruction in Thumb mode, which can cause issues on some ARM targets.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14647](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14647) **Missing GC root for continuation in `reperform`** opened by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) as a fix for the interpreter's handling of effects.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14636](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14636) **Ban unsafe primitive aliases inside recursive modules** proposed by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) to prevent potential unsoundness.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14642](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14642) **Runtime cleanup-at-exit** improvement proposed by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): `caml_shutdown` would be ignored rather than cause errors when other domains remain running.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml/ocaml#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634) [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened a discussion on **what should be the default minor heap size**, noting that the current 256Kword (2MiB) default has not changed since 2011 and that allocation-heavy benchmarks with `OCAMLRUNPARAM=s=2M` often show 2x speedups. The discussion explores sizing relative to L3 cache or total RAM.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14644](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14644) [Thibaut Mattio](https://github.com/tmattio) reported **bytecode heap corruption** when using `try Effect.perform with Effect.Unhandled` under a deep effect handler, a likely regression related to continuation representation changes in [ocaml/ocaml#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14639](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14639) [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) reported a bug where a **labelled argument of a labelled tuple gets its labels confused** in the typechecker.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14630) [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) reported a regression in 5.5 where **"The signature for this packaged module couldn't be inferred"** is incorrectly raised in the presence of recursive functions and first-class modules.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594) Ongoing discussion around **segfaults and timeouts on parallel tests** in cleanup-at-exit mode, tracked by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) with a proposed fix via [ocaml/ocaml#14642](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14642) from [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Bytecode backend as a vehicle for JavaScript/WebAssembly optimization**: [ocaml/ocaml#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438) adds a new `HINT` section to bytecode files so tools like Js_of_ocaml can access Lambda-level information (closure kinds, C call details, mutability) that was previously unavailable at the bytecode level, controlled by a new `-bytecode-hints` flag.
- **Modular explicits** continue active development with [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)'s work on eliding optional arguments in the Lambda IR ([ocaml/ocaml#14648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14648)) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)'s refactoring of `Typeopt.is_function_type` ([ocaml/ocaml#14631](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14631), [ocaml/ocaml#14632](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14632)) to handle `Tfunctor`.
- **Effects and continuations stability**: both the heap corruption bug ([ocaml/ocaml#14644](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14644)) and the missing GC root in `reperform` ([ocaml/ocaml#14647](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14647)) point to ongoing hardening of the effects runtime.
- **Typing precision improvements** from [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) (University of Cambridge) continue with multiple open PRs addressing let-rec approximation, polymorphic patterns, and warning 16 correctness.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Eio saw filesystem API proposals from a contributor; all other multicore repos were quiet this week.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was confined to [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio); the remaining eight repositories (domainslib, ocaml-uring, meio, saturn, eio-trace, picos, kcas, ocaml-iomux) recorded no new PRs, issues, or commits
- Two open items in eio are awaiting merge/resolution: [ocaml-multicore/eio#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833) (approved PR adding `missing_ok` to `Eio.Path.unlink`) and [ocaml-multicore/eio#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831) (proposal for tempdir utilities)
- Both items were authored by [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) and reviewed by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)
group_summaries.activity_summary: PR ocaml-multicore/eio#833 adding missing_ok to Eio.Path.unlink is approved and awaiting merge; tempdir proposal #831 is in design discussion.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) opened [ocaml-multicore/eio#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833) adding an optional `missing_ok` parameter to `Eio.Path.unlink`, allowing callers to silently ignore missing-file errors; the PR addresses [ocaml-multicore/eio#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828), is small (8 additions, 3 deletions across 2 files), and has been approved by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) also opened [ocaml-multicore/eio#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831) proposing `Eio.Stdenv.tmp`, `Eio.Path.open_temp_dir`, and `Eio.Path.with_temp_dir` convenience APIs; design feedback from [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) is shaping the direction of the proposal
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) responded to the tempdir proposal [ocaml-multicore/eio#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831) noting that `Eio` must not depend on `eio.unix`, so `run_in_systhread` is not directly usable; he suggested routing the temp-directory path through `Stdenv` for backend portability (covering `/tmp`, `$TMPDIR`, and the Windows `GetTempPath` equivalent), and raised whether cleanup should be opt-out rather than always automatic
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) approved [ocaml-multicore/eio#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833), commenting "this looks good to me"
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) is contributing a set of filesystem convenience utilities to eio — `missing_ok` for unlink ([ocaml-multicore/eio#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833)) and tempdir lifecycle management ([ocaml-multicore/eio#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831)) — filling gaps users currently handle manually via `Eio.Stdenv.fs` and `Filename` from the standard library
- Backend portability is a recurring design constraint in eio API additions, as seen in the tempdir discussion ([ocaml-multicore/eio#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831)) where Unix-specific implementation paths must be avoided to keep the API usable across all eio backends

weekly_summary.brief_summary: Dune 3.22.0~alpha1 released; OxCaml saw 28 PRs with -O4 level and reaper advances; bytecode hints merged in compiler for Js_of_ocaml.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: Bytecode hints for Js_of_ocaml, new -O4 level with reaper, dune sandboxing-by-default, js_of_ocaml OCaml 5.5 alpha, and olint linter released.
weekly_summary.new_features:
**Compiler (ocaml/ocaml)**
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438) **Bytecode optimization hints** merged: the bytecode compiler now preserves optimization hints from the Lambda IR (closure kinds, immutable blocks, array lengths, C call details) in a new `HINT` section in `.cmo` and `.cma` files, enabled with `-bytecode-hints`. This allows [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)'s Js_of_ocaml and Wasm_of_ocaml to access type-level information previously discarded at the bytecode stage.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14618](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14618) **Faster UTF-8 normalization** outside the ASCII range merged, optimizing the `Misc.Stdlib` unicode normalization path. Contributed by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), reviewed by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), Daniel Bünzli, and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).

**OxCaml (oxcaml/oxcaml)**
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml#5574](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5574) A new **`-O4` optimization level** was introduced, bundling the reaper dead-code elimination pass with `-O3`; the `[@flambda_o4]` attribute mirrors it at the per-function level.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml#5610](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5610) The **`[@unpacked]` attribute** for C stub externals allows unboxed product types as C stub arguments by flattening each product component into separate native C arguments, while bytecode stubs receive a single boxed value.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml#5597](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5597) A **CFG block merging pass** (`-cfg-merge-blocks`) was merged, deduplicating equivalent basic blocks after register allocation, achieving up to 4.5% reduction in instruction count with only 0.2% build-time overhead.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Constant propagation** in the CFG backend extended to cover integer arithmetic ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5545](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5545)), floating-point arithmetic ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5546](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5546)), and values inferred from predecessor terminators ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5562](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5562)) by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml#5579](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5579) **`Multicore.spawn_result`** now accepts `value_or_null` resources, aligning the multicore API with the null-safety story.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml#5629](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5629) **`with kind_` constraints** added as part of abstract kinds part 3, completing the core feature set for abstract kinds.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml#5628](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5628) **`ocamldep` now scans `with kind` annotations**, ensuring correct dependency tracking for modules using abstract kinds.

**Tools**
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocaml/dune#13805](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13805) **Sandboxing enabled by default** for user rules from dune lang 3.23, catching undeclared dependencies at build time.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocaml/dune#13341](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13341) **Asynchronous digest computation** (part 2) — target files and directories now digested in background threads, reducing main build loop blocking.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613) **Jsoo config management** moved out of dune, reducing the need for dune to track all possible js_of_ocaml configs upfront.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177) New **`--build-config` and `--apply-build-config` CLI flags** for both `js_of_ocaml` and `wasm_of_ocaml`, enabling coherent multi-unit compilation by printing and overriding build-relevant compiler settings.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2197](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2197) **OCaml 5.5.0~alpha1 support** added to js_of_ocaml, including new JS and Wasm runtime primitives for `gc`, `sys`, and `domain` modules.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2198](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2198) **Shape computation performance overhauled** with coinductive caching in `Flow.the_shape_of`, fixing potential exponential blowups — a pathological case dropped from >7 hours to ~200ms compilation time.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2191](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2191) **Binaryen version check** added to the `wasm_of_ocaml` runtime build, providing a clear error on missing `wasm-merge` when Binaryen < 119.

**Ecosystem (opam-repository)**
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/opam-repository#29543](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29543) **olint.0.1.0** — new OCaml linter inspired by Rust's Clippy, checking 10 antipattern rules using compiler-libs on the untyped AST, by [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/opam-repository#29523](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29523) **oui.0.1.0** — a tool that builds standalone installers for OCaml applications, by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/opam-repository#29531](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29531) **libinput.0.1** — initial release of OCaml bindings for the libinput input device library, by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/opam-repository#29526](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29526) **parseff.0.1.0** — direct-style parser combinator library for OCaml 5 powered by algebraic effects, by [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/ocaml.org#3558](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3558) The **Installing OCaml** and **Configuring Your Editor** tutorials updated to mention the OCaml devcontainer as a zero-install path, with a new **Dev Containers** section added to the Docker Images tutorial, by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/ocaml.org#3556](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3556) A new **`release-scrapers.yml` GitHub Actions workflow** introduced by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) to build and publish the scraper binary as a `scraper-latest` release, eliminating source recompilation on every scrape run.

weekly_summary.activity_summary: 9 PRs merged in compiler, 28 in oxcaml/oxcaml, 46 dune commits, js_of_ocaml Wasm maturation, and ~30 opam packages across the ecosystem.
weekly_summary.activity:
**Core (ocaml/ocaml)**
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14626) **Warning 16 false positive** with module-dependent functions fixed by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob): the typechecker now correctly handles function types with module-dependent parameters when determining whether an optional argument is unerasable.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14638](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14638) **`is_inferred` now traverses local opens** correctly, fixing the issue reported in [ocaml/ocaml#14629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14629). Merged by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14635) **`caml_floatarray_gather` control flow bug** fixed by [Marc Lasson](https://github.com/mlasson): `Float.Array.sub`, `Float.Array.append`, and `Float.Array.concat` could return a non-empty array when the result should have been `[||]`.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14645](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14645) **Ephemeron GC corner case** documented by [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) to prevent future confusion around ephemeron marking during the major GC.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14617](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14617) The **`ocaml_deprecated_cli` alert** is now made fatal in compiler development flags by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), enforcing migration away from deprecated CLI patterns.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14646](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14646) **ARM Thumb mode** fix opened by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) to avoid using the `TBH` (Table Branch Halfword) instruction in Thumb mode, which can cause issues on some ARM targets.

weekly_summary.cross_repository_work:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune → js_of_ocaml unblocking**: the jsoo config management refactor ([ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613)) merged this week directly unblocks the js_of_ocaml WASI runtime PR ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)), which listed it as a blocking dependency. [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) contributed to both [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) (jsoo config management, trace file fix [ocaml/dune#13735](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13735)) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) (5 merged PRs including [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2198](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2198)).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune 3.22 → ocaml-lsp breakage**: the `dune-rpc` API change broke `ocaml-lsp-server` (discussed in [ocaml/dune#13674](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13674)); [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) opened [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1594) to fix ocaml-lsp's build, and [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) confirmed the break was deliberate and plans a separate LSP compatibility fix.
- __RUMINANT:core__ __RUMINANT:tools__ **Bytecode hints bridging compiler and js_of_ocaml**: [ocaml/ocaml#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438) in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) adds a new `HINT` section to bytecode files specifically to enable [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) and wasm_of_ocaml to access Lambda-level optimization data, representing coordinated cross-repository work between [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) and the OCaml compiler team.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **ppxlib 5.5 preview** spans both groups: [ocaml/opam-repository#29507](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29507) published `ppxlib.0.38.0~5.5preview` for the upcoming OCaml 5.5 release by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb), while [ocaml/ocaml.org#3560](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3560) cleaned up the ppxlib developer meeting entry from governance data.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OxCaml SIMD content** appeared across multiple repos: [ocaml/opam-repository#29490](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29490) raven.1.0.0~alpha3 introduced an nx-oxcaml SIMD tensor backend, while the OCaml Planet feed on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) ingested posts on an OxCaml SIMD ONNX inference engine and Pi Day benchmarks.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune 3.22.0~alpha1 in opam-repository**: [ocaml/opam-repository#29535](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29535) is the draft opam-repository PR for the dune alpha release, with a separate constraint PR [ocaml/opam-repository#29544](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29544) addressing the `dune-rpc` API break affecting downstream packages including `ocaml-lsp-server`.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Windows portability coordination**: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s review of the Windows `_WIN32_WINNT` fix in opam ([ocaml/opam#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862)) referenced related work in [ocaml/ocaml#14484](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14484), indicating coordinated Windows portability effort across the OCaml toolchain.

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634) [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened a discussion on **what should be the default minor heap size**, noting that the current 256Kword (2MiB) default has not changed since 2011 and that allocation-heavy benchmarks with `OCAMLRUNPARAM=s=2M` often show 2x speedups. The discussion explores sizing relative to L3 cache or total RAM.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14644](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14644) [Thibaut Mattio](https://github.com/tmattio) reported **bytecode heap corruption** when using `try Effect.perform with Effect.Unhandled` under a deep effect handler, a likely regression related to continuation representation changes in [ocaml/ocaml#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469).
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14630) [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) reported a regression in 5.5 where **"The signature for this packaged module couldn't be inferred"** is incorrectly raised in the presence of recursive functions and first-class modules.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608) [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) and [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) debated whether the `write`/`observable` **mode diamond** should use separate non-diamond axes internally for performance; [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) demonstrated that a bit-vector encoding makes the diamond approach equally efficient.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune RPC API compatibility** ([ocaml/dune#13674](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13674)): the 3.22.0 release saw discussion about an intentional `dune-rpc` API change that breaks `ocaml-lsp-server`; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) confirmed the break was deliberate and plans to fix LSP compatibility separately.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **js_of_ocaml shape computation performance** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2198](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2198)): discussion around a 15% increase in `ocamlc` compilation time from the fix, with [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) adding an extra optimization round; [Ricky Vetter](https://github.com/rickyvetter) confirmed a real-world pathological case dropped from >7 hours to ~200ms.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/opam-repository#29530](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29530) **lwt 6 constraint refinements** — open PR adjusting upper bounds across ~84 files after lwt 6 removed long-deprecated APIs (`Lwt_main.exit_hooks`, `Lwt_unix.run`, abstract `Lwt_engine`); [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) noted 440 downstream failures with lwt.6.1.1, by [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/opam-repository#29072](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29072) **Stricter immutability policy** merged — strengthens the rule that published package tarballs must not change, now a hard enforcement in opam-repo-ci lint; this directly triggered the tm-grammars 1.0.0 checksum situation and the decision to release 1.0.1 instead of patching, by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm).
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ [ocaml-multicore/eio#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831) [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) responded to the **tempdir proposal** noting that `Eio` must not depend on `eio.unix`, so `run_in_systhread` is not directly usable; he suggested routing the temp-directory path through `Stdenv` for backend portability, and raised whether cleanup should be opt-out rather than always automatic.

weekly_summary.emerging_trends:
- __RUMINANT:core__ __RUMINANT:tools__ **Bytecode backend as vehicle for JS/WebAssembly optimization**: [ocaml/ocaml#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438) adds a `HINT` section to bytecode files so [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) can access Lambda-level information (closure kinds, C call details, mutability) previously unavailable at the bytecode level, reflecting a trend of treating the bytecode backend as an IR bridge for non-native compilation targets.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ The **reaper pass** is stabilizing rapidly, with fixes for local fields, zero_alloc interactions, error messages, and unboxed arguments ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5593](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5593), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5611](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5611), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5619](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5619)) suggesting it is approaching production readiness at `-O4` as introduced in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5574](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5574).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Abstract kinds** are nearing feature completeness with recursive module support ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5517](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5517)) and `with kind_` constraints ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5629](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5629)) now implemented, setting up for a broader public announcement of the feature.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ The **mode system** continues to expand in parallel tracks: `write`/`observable` modes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)), external modality in with-bounds ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5601](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5601)), and improved syntax for mode constraints without a type ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5602](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5602)), all progressing simultaneously.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **CFG backend infrastructure** around block equality and constant propagation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5002](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5002), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5545](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5545), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5546](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5546), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5562](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5562)) is being built out systematically by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc), enabling increasingly sophisticated optimizations in the pipeline.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Wasm backend maturation in js_of_ocaml**: concurrent PRs for dynlink/toplevel support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)), WASI runtime ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)), and full OCaml 5.5 compatibility ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195)) indicate sustained investment in the Wasm compilation target, continuing from last week's OCaml 5.5 support work.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Sandboxing and build correctness**: dune 3.23's sandboxing-by-default ([ocaml/dune#13805](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13805)) and async digest pipeline ([ocaml/dune#13341](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13341)) continue the trend toward stricter, more reliable build guarantees, reducing the class of undeclared-dependency bugs that reach users.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Reproducibility infrastructure tightening** across the ecosystem: the merged immutability policy in opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29072](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29072)) and the switch to a pre-built scraper binary on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3556](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3556), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3557](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3557)) both reflect a broader push toward deterministic, reproducible workflows.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Relocatability groundwork**: the `relocatable.packages` virtual package ([ocaml/opam-repository#29451](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29451)) sets up infrastructure for Dune's package management mode to enforce switch portability on OCaml 5.5+, continuing the theme of cross-repo coordination for next-generation package management.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Effects and continuations runtime hardening**: both the bytecode heap corruption bug ([ocaml/ocaml#14644](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14644)) and the missing GC root in `reperform` ([ocaml/ocaml#14647](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14647)) point to ongoing stabilization work on the effects runtime, reinforcing the pattern from previous weeks of systematic effects correctness improvements.
